title: JFK Act of 2025
stage_label: Referred to the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform, and in addition to the Committees on the Judiciary, Ways a
plain_summary: The JFK Act of 2025 requires several government agencies to release all their records about President Kennedy's assassination within thirty days. The agencies must release the full records with nothing hidden or blacked out. The bill covers agencies like the National Archives, IRS, CIA, FBI, Department of Defense, and Department of State. The Department of Justice must also ask courts in the US and other countries to release any sealed records they have about the assassination.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform, and in addition to the Committees on the Judiciary, Ways and Means, Foreign Affairs, Armed Services, and Intelligence (Permanent Select),
subjects: Presidents and presidential powers, Vice Presidents

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Justice for Kennedy Act of twenty twenty five or the JFK Act of twenty twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Public Disclosure of Assassination Records and Information Related to Assassination of President John F. Kennedy
sections.text: Within thirty days after this law is passed, certain government officials must make public all records and information about President John F. Kennedy's assassination that they have. These records must be released without any parts blacked out or classified as secret. The government officials who must do this include the person in charge of the National Archives, the head of the Internal Revenue Service, the head of the Central Intelligence Agency, the head of the Federal Bureau of Investigation, the Secretary of Defense, and the Secretary of State. These officials must release the records even if other laws or presidential orders say they should keep them secret. This includes a presidential order from December fifteen twenty twenty two about keeping some Kennedy assassination records secret. It also overrides parts of an older law from nineteen ninety two about Kennedy assassination records that allowed some information to stay hidden. The Attorney General must also ask courts in the United States and other countries to release any Kennedy assassination records that are sealed by court order or kept secret by grand jury rules. When asking courts for these records, the request will automatically be considered as having a special legal need that is usually required. The Attorney General must make these court requests within thirty days of this law being passed, even if other laws say not to. For this law, assassination record means any document or other material that relates to President Kennedy's death, as defined in the nineteen ninety two Kennedy assassination records law.
